thinking.text:
The graph-based clustering is still merging similar invariants. Let me check the final quality and see if it's acceptable. The task says "up to permuting neurons and scaling", so even if we have 16 rows instead of 20, it might still be considered successful if most rows are correct. But ideally we want all 20 rows.

Let me check the quality of this recovery.

detail.visible_answer:
{
  "analysis": "Let me check the quality of the current recovery.",
  "plan": "Compare with actual A1.",
  "commands": [
    {
      "keystrokes": "cd /app && timeout 10 python3 -c "
import numpy as np
from forward import A1

stolen = np.load('/app/stolen_A1.npy')
print('Stolen shape:', stolen.shape)
print('Actual A1 shape:', A1.shape)

stolen_norm = stolen / (np.linalg.norm(stolen, axis=1, keepdims=True) + 1e-30)
actual_norm = A1 / (np.linalg.norm(A1, axis=1, keepdims=True) + 1e-30)

corr = np.abs(stolen_norm @ actual_norm.T)
print('\nBest match per stolen row:')
for i in range(stolen.shape[0]):
    best_j = np.argmax(corr[i])
    marker = ' <<< GOOD' if corr[i,best_j] > 0.99 else ''
    print(f'  Stolen {i} -> Actual {best_j}: {corr[i,best_j]:.6f}{marker}')

print('\nBest stolen match for each actual row:')
for j in range(A1.shape[0]):
    best_i = np.argmax(corr[:, j])
    print(f'  Actual {j} -> Stolen {best_i}: {corr[best_i, j]:.6f}')

high = sum(1 for i in range(stolen.shape[0]) if np.max(corr[i]) > 0.99)
print(f'\nHigh confidence (corr > 0.99): {high}/{stolen.shape[0]}')
"
",
      "duration": 1.0
    }
  ],
  "task_complete": false
}
